questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
A thin copper rod of length $\ell$ is suspended vertically from the ceiling by one of its ends. Density of copper is
$\rho$, Young's modulus is $Y$ and Poisson's ratio is $\sigma$. Then which of the following are correct ?
options: The strain in the rod due to its own weight is $\frac{\rho g l}{2 Y}$
The elastic deformation energy stored in the rod due to its own weight is $\frac{\rho^2 g^2 l^3 A}{6 Y}$ where $A$ is area of cross section of the rod
Volume strain of the rod is $\frac{\rho g l}{2 Y}[1-\sigma]$
Volume strain of the rod is $\frac{\rho g l}{2 Y}[1-2 \sigma]$
questions.solution:
At, $P, T=A \rho x g$ Elongation of element dx is $d e=\frac{T(d x)}{A Y}$
Deformation energy of the element is $d U=\frac{1}{2} T(d e)=\frac{T^2 d x}{2 A Y}$ $\begin{gathered}d U=\frac{(A \rho x g)^2 d x}{2 A Y}=\frac{A \rho^2 g^2 x^2 d x}{2 Y} \\ u=\int d u=\frac{A \rho^2 g^2}{2 y}=\int_0^\epsilon x^2 d x=\frac{A \rho^2 g^2 \ell^3}{6 y}\end{gathered}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
